TypeError: MeanAbsoluteError.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'y_true'

llm_operation.reason: PyTorch torch.nn.L1Loss is a loss class that does not accept 'input' or 'target' in __init__ — those are passed later to the __call__ method. Similarly, TensorFlow tf.keras.losses.MeanAbsoluteError is instantiated without arguments and called with y_true/y_pred. The original test cases incorrectly pass tensor specs (shape/dtype) as constructor arguments, causing 'unexpected keyword argument' errors. Repair involves moving tensor specifications out of constructor args and instead specifying them for the forward/call invocation — but since this test format expects constructor-style JSON, we must instead use valid constructor-only parameters (e.g., reduction) and remove invalid ones ('input', 'target', 'y_true', 'y_pred'). Both APIs accept only reduction-related args in __init__: PyTorch accepts 'reduction', TensorFlow accepts no args (or optionally 'name', but 'reduction' is not supported). So we repair by providing only valid constructor args: for PyTorch, 'reduction'='mean'; for TensorFlow, no args (empty object), as it has no constructor parameters per doc.
